a plump ump rump.example_clue: Bodacious booty of a baseball adjudicator

a scary hairy fairy.example_clue: A creature like Tinkerbell whose hirsuteness causes quite the fright

a lame frame name.example_clue: Uncool moniker for your new painting enclosure

erring bering herring (erring herring be.example_clue: Mistake-making pickled fish from the sea named for Vitus

seize the chinese trees.example_clue: An order to forcefully take the Shanghai maples

tarot cards.example_clue: The Lovers, the Wheel of Fortune & the Knight of Wands are examples of these cards

umami.example_clue: The word for this flavor sometimes called the fifth basic taste comes from Japanese for "deliciousness"

salty.example_clue: This adjective can describe your upset friend after you roast them; it also describes the taste of Old Bay seasoning

taiga.example_clue: It's a Russian word for a boreal forest with many pine trees

a dramatization.example_clue: It's the specific word for a novel or story adapted for the theater, like Craig Walker's stage version of "Finnegans Wake"

nonconfrontational.example_clue: Because I'm this adjective, I really don't want to argue; I'm pretty sure the word has 5 "N"s but if you disagree, I'll walk away

mostaccioli.example_clue: Penne rigate has ridges; penne lisce is smooth & is also known as this, a real mouthful, especially good with tomato sauce

contraindication.example_clue: 6-syllable word for a symptom that tells a doctor not to prescribe a certain drug to a patient

a semiquincentennial.example_clue: It's the word for a 250th, half of 500th, birthday & for the special coins the U.S. Mint is issuing for our country's

a patsy.example_clue: The name of a naive stage character, this diminutive of Patrick came to mean a fall guy or rube (hey, there's another one!)

jerry.example_clue: It can precede "built" to mean something constructed cheaply & badly

brandy.example_clue: It's a fine girl's name as well as a class of fine distilled beverages like cognac & armagnac

a barb.example_clue: A cut-down form of a female name, it can also mean a cutting remark or a cutting part of a fishhook

a derrick.example_clue: A hoisting apparatus, or a support tower over a drill hole

vatican city of brotherly love.example_clue: Papal realm that's a nickname for Philadelphia

the golden gate bridge of sighs.display: the Golden Gate Bridge of Sighs
the golden gate bridge of sighs.example_clue: Span that links Marin County to San Francisco while also leading to the doge's palace

key west end.example_clue: Florida city connected to the mainland by the Overseas Highway that's home to the London Theatre District

the bering strait of messina.example_clue: Waterway that links the Pacific to the Arctic Ocean while separating Sicily from Italy

oak park place.example_clue: Suburb of Chicago with many Frank Lloyd Wright homes that's one of the most valuable properties in Monopoly

spotify.example_clue: S: Wrapped is its Year-in-Review feature

dirt devil.example_clue: DD: In 1984 its new hand vac really sucked

bose.example_clue: B: Speakers with headquarters in Framingham, Massachusetts

quadrennially.example_clue: An event that occurs every 4 years happens this adverbial way

insubordinate.example_clue: "I" know if you're disobedient or disrespectful to authority, you're this adjective, soldier!

precipitation.example_clue: It can mean both hastiness & rain, for example

pontificating.example_clue: You're not the pope, so stop doing this, speaking in a pompous or dogmatic manner

paterfamilias.example_clue: Latin word for the head of a family; in law it follows "diligens" to mean a theoretical person who takes proper care

sports illustrated.example_clue: Turns out this mag with cover subjects like Lynn Swann (1976) & Candace Parker (2007) used A.I.-generated articles in 2023

good housekeeping.example_clue: Its Seal of Approval dates back to 1909

reader's digest.example_clue: It now publishes a large-print edition to go along with its familiar 5.5-inch by 7.5-inch version

national geographic.example_clue: Under editor Gilbert Grosvenor, this magazine known for maps & photos reached a circulation of 1 million by 1926

harper's bazaar.example_clue: This fashion magazine was founded in 1867 with Mary Louise Booth as its editor; the double-A spelling came in 1929

birthstone.example_clue: Aquamarine is the one for March; September's is sapphire

gesundheit.example_clue: This word, German for "health", is nothing to sneeze at... or is it?

equestrian.example_clue: This fancy adjective for horse riding describes certain Olympic events

apothecary.example_clue: It's a synonym for pharmacy, or a term for one who prepares & sells medicinal drugs

indigenous.example_clue: If we may go native, this adjective refers to someone born in a specific region

penultimate.example_clue: It means next to last in a series

blep.example_clue: Used affectionately, this slangy 4-letter word is when an animal has its tongue hanging partway out

a lycanthrope.example_clue: Derived from Greek, it's the technical term for a werewolf

gossamer.example_clue: It can describe a thin, light fabric, or be a film of cobwebs floating in the air

akimbo.example_clue: The person seen here has their arms in this position

salt lake city.example_clue: This state capital is found between Ogden to the north & Provo to the south

rio de janeiro.example_clue: Surf's up at Prainha, a gorgeous little beach in this Brazilian city

san juan capistrano.example_clue: It 's where Southern California swallows return every March

port-au-prince.example_clue: This Haitian city's National Palace collapsed in 2010 due to a big earthquake

dar es salaam.example_clue: Arabic for "abode of peace", it's Tanzania's biggest city

unfinished business.example_clue: What you might still have with that bully from grade school BUSINES

buy low, sell high.example_clue: A good strategy when buying stocks SELL BUY

the good, the bad and the ugly.display: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly
the good, the bad and the ugly.example_clue: A classic Sergio Leone film SAMARITAN APPLE & DUCKLING

bend over backwards.example_clue: To make an extraordinary exertion BEND REVO REVO

a foreign language.example_clue: Good to learn before your next vacation, perhaps SWA4HILI

abstinence.example_clue: Early Carmelites vowed austerity & this other "A" word referring to refraining from a certain desire

decimation.example_clue: In Latin times this noun referred to the punishing of every tenth man; now it refers to any wholesale slaughter

johnnycake.example_clue: This breakfast food cooked on the griddle begins with a boy's name

ruminating.example_clue: If you're doing this, you're either chewing cud or pondering the world

gastrology.example_clue: Add one letter to the practice of divining omens from the stars & you get this, the study of the stomach

a kleptomaniac.example_clue: It's a person with a compulsive urge to cop, crib or boost

ambidextrous.example_clue: Though he favored his left, James Garfield was this, from words meaning "right-handed on both sides"

instrumental.example_clue: This word can refer to music played without vocals or, in another sense, anything helpful to a cause

pumpernickel.example_clue: This dark bread is made with rye flour & can be substituted for rye bread in sandwiches

salutatorian.example_clue: It's the student with the second-highest ranking in a high school class

a bun nub.example_clue: A protuberance on one's bread roll

suez zeus.example_clue: A Greek deity visiting a port city in Northern Egypt

shaun white elephant.example_clue: A legendary redheaded snowboarder is given as a gift but sadly becomes an expensive inconvenience

covalent bond, james bond.example_clue: The sharing of an electron pair between 2 atoms becomes even more unique when agent 007 gives his clichéd introduction

latin quarter pounder.example_clue: Paris district that's home to the St. Michel Fountain & doubles as a beefy burger at McDonald's

relationship status quo.example_clue: An indication on Facebook of whether you're taken, followed by a Latin phrase for "the way things are"

public defender of the faith.example_clue: A lawyer who represents those who can't pay on their own & the title given by Pope Leo X to the British sovereign

a clam tam.example_clue: A woolen cap for a bivalve

waste paste.example_clue: To use far more glue than is necessary

ponder yonder.example_clue: To really think about what's over there, thataway

anchor rancor.example_clue: Deep-seated ill will among news broadcasters

a revolver.example_clue: The guns made by Samuel Colt acquired this name from the way the cylinder turned

a portuguese man o' war.example_clue: This jellyfish relative is named for its resemblance to an old combat ship with sails

escalate.example_clue: This 8-letter verb meaning to increase was created after the invention of a moving staircase

pedigree.example_clue: This word comes from French genealogical charts -- the branching lines looked like the pied or foot of a bird

foosball.example_clue: The name of this game is basically a German word minus Tisch, or "table"

wolves.example_clue: Thomas Jefferson: A monarchy "is a government of" these "over sheep"

the constitution.example_clue: John Adams: "Avarice, ambition, revenge... would break the strongest cords of" this document "as a whale goes through a net"

a debt.example_clue: Alexander Hamilton, who knew about handling money: "A national" this, "if it is not excessive will be to us a national blessing"

the federalist papers.example_clue: James Madison, in No. 47 of these papers: Tyranny is "legislative, executive, and judiciary, in the same hands"

george mason.example_clue: This man for whom a Fairfax, Virginia university is named said the slave trade was "disgraceful to mankind"

fever.example_clue: Coloring your world in a not-so-great way, it follows scarlet & yellow

hives.example_clue: Bee aware this skin reaction is also called urticaria

ebola.example_clue: Claiming 11,000 lives before containment, the worst-ever outbreak of this virus plagued West Africa from 2014 to 2016

lupus.example_clue: Guess what, TV's "House"? It is in fact this auto-immune disorder; the discoid type affects only the skin, not organs

croup.example_clue: The HIB vaccine is highly effective vs. bacterial this acute respiratory illness, which tends to strike kids 7 or younger

a stock stock.example_clue: A supply of broth kept on hand

a conjunction.example_clue: In a "Schoolhouse Rock!" song, this part of speech is "hookin' up words & phrases & clauses"

the magnificent.example_clue: This impressive epithet graced a cinematic septet & an Ottoman Suleyman

dysfunction.example_clue: Some late-night TV ads mention items delivered "discreetly" & this word, erected from words meaning "bad" & "performance"

a barnstormer.example_clue: Showcasing aviation excitement, it's someone traveling from place to place flying & performing aerial stunts

imagination.example_clue: Albert Einstein once said this creative ability "is more important than knowledge"

an attaché.example_clue: A specialized officer with an embassy may be a liaison or this; you can be a military one or perhaps an agricultural one

a hammock.example_clue: Quilted & rope are types of this backyard swinger

a phalanx.example_clue: "X" marks the last letter of this ancient Macedonian tactical formation

an acolyte.example_clue: It can refer to a person assisting a priest celebrating mass, or to a devoted follower

a brazier.example_clue: From French, it's a portable heater on a stand with a pan for holding hot coals

sultan.example_clue: Around 1000 A.D. Mahmud of Ghazna was the 1st Muslim ruler to be called this title; this "of Swat" was a much different guy

a succubus.example_clue: Time to throw you (figuratively!) under this female type of promiscuous demon

sugarcoat.example_clue: Give it to me straight -- don't do this 9-letter verb that means to try to sweeten an unpleasant situation

suet.example_clue: It's the solid white fat around sheep's kidneys

subclavicular (subclavian).example_clue: This adjective means beneath the collarbone

x.example_clue: The man who founded Boston Temple No. 11 in the 1950s before moving to No. 7 in Harlem

g.example_clue: The musician who somehow took a jazz album to No. 2 on both the Billboard 200 and R&B/Hip-Hop charts

o.example_clue: The historic figure in the N.Y. Post headline that reported her "childhood home asks $44M at 740 Park Ave" in 2014

w.example_clue: The co-founder of a self-help group whose wife Lois co-founded Al-Anon

k.example_clue: The 1925 literary character who "was informed by telephone that there would be a small hearing concerning his case"

baron & bacon.example_clue: The lowest rank of nobility & a delicious breakfast meat

encourage & entourage.example_clue: To inspire & your retinue, maybe including Turtle & Johnny Drama

the doge & the loge.example_clue: Old title for a Venetian official & seating section in an opera house

a miser & a miter.example_clue: A stingy sort & a tall hat for a bishop

aide.example_clue: You can get this word meaning helper by adding an "E" to a verb meaning help

gird.example_clue: To prepare oneself for action, loins & all

lede.example_clue: The spelling of this word for part of a news article was meant to prevent confusion with one of its homophones

twee.example_clue: This mainly British word once meant adorably dainty but now signifies overly precious & cute

mete.example_clue: This word means to dole something out in portions, like, say, sausages or turkey legs

copenhagen.example_clue: This city is located on the islands of Zealand & Amager just west of Malmö, Sweden

wellington.example_clue: Mount Victoria rises over 600 feet above this capital at the southern end of North Island

montevideo.example_clue: A port city on the Rio de la Plata, it's the southernmost capital in South America

addis ababa.example_clue: Empress Taitu, the wife of Menelik II, had a house built at the site that would become this world capital

bratislava.example_clue: Rick Steves says this Danube city was a "damaged husk" under communism; it revived since 1993 & becoming the capital of its own country

heart.example_clue: In the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame: Center; Ticker

wham!.example_clue: Formed in 1981 by schoolmates: Thud!; Smack!
